perturbation.original_text: "Approved Merchant" means any Merchant that: (a) Is solicited by ISO (or ISO's Other MSP's or IC's) for participation in the Program; (b) Meets the established criteria for participation in the Program, including the Merchant Processing Policy; and (c) Enters into a Merchant Processing Agreement. Approved Merchants may also include Merchants converted to the Program as part of the initial BIN/ICA transfer and/or subsequently acquired Merchants that meet the established criteria for participation in the Program, including the Merchant Processing Policy, and whose sponsorship is assigned to Bank.
perturbation.changed_text: "Approved Merchant" means any Merchant that: (a) Is solicited by ISO (or ISO's Other MSP's or IC's) for participation in the Program; (b) Enters into a Merchant Processing Agreement; and (c) Has been actively processing transactions for a minimum of 6 months. Approved Merchants may also include Merchants converted to the Program as part of the initial BIN/ICA transfer and/or subsequently acquired Merchants whose sponsorship is assigned to Bank.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Approved Merchant'. Initially, an 'Approved Merchant' had to meet established criteria, including the Merchant Processing Policy. The revised definition removes this requirement, replacing it with a minimum transaction processing period of 6 months. This contradicts the original intent as it could include merchants who do not meet the Merchant Processing Policy guidelines but have been processing transactions for at least six months. This is contradictory in Section 4.2 Underwriting. ISO shall underwrite, perform a credit review and conduct a site inspection, when applicable, for each Application as required by this Agreement, the Merchant Processing Policy and the Rules to determine whether each Applicant is an Eligible Merchant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Underwriting. ISO shall underwrite, perform a credit review and conduct a site inspection, when applicable, for each Application as required by this Agreement, the Merchant Processing Policy and the Rules to determine whether each Applicant is an Eligible Merchant. ISO may conduct such credit review without notification to and participation by SERVICERS, except as otherwise provided below (i.e., for any *** Omitted pursuant to a confidential treatment request. The confidential portion has been filed separately with the SEC. Page 11 prospective Merchant that falls within the risk and volume parameters set forth in this Section 4.2), provided that ISO performs the credit review in accordance with the Merchant Processing Policy, the terms of this Agreement and the Rules. In accordance with and as permitted by this Agreement, a third-party service provider approved by SERVICERS may perform site inspections for ISO. In order to assist ISO in its underwriting hereunder, SERVICERS shall assist ISO with checking Merchants and prospective Merchants against the Terminated Merchant File/MATCH List or any similar list maintained by any entity. ISO shall be responsible for all credit and fraud losses associated with the Merchant Portfolio or the Program, regardless of any services or assistance that may be provided by SERVICERS. For any high risk Merchant who in good faith ISO anticipates will have annual Visa and MasterCard transaction volume equal to or greater than $[***] and any low risk Merchant who in good faith ISO anticipates will have annual Visa and MasterCard transaction volume equal to or greater than $[***], ISO shall advise SERVICERS and permit SERVICERS to conduct their own credit review prior to boarding the account. Upon receipt of a complete underwriting package from ISO, SERVICERS will respond to ISO within [***] [***] Business Days as to SERVICERS' acceptance or rejection of such Merchant. SERVICERS' participation in any credit review will not in any way relieve ISO from its responsibility for credit and fraud losses which may result from a Merchant's transaction processing. SERVICERS may at any time require that a Merchant relationship be terminated by ISO and Bank may cease the provision of services to any such Merchant pursuant to the terms of the Merchant Processing Agreement. SERVICERS shall have the right and authority to accept or reject any Application. ISO shall obtain prospective Merchants' authorization for ISO and/or SERVICERS to obtain all reports (including personal and business credit reports) and other information necessary in connection with the Application. ISO shall have the right, subject to and pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Merchant Processing Policy, to cause any Eligible Merchant with transaction card volume processing limits up to but not more than $[***] per month, that has been approved by ISO, to be activated immediately in SERVICES' systems. SERVICERS agree to provide ISO with a final approval decision (i.e., acceptance, rejection or required modification of the submitted Application) on submitted and complete Merchant Applications for Eligible Merchants that are not classified as "high risk" on the High Risk Merchant List attached hereto as Exhibit F and which seek approval for transaction card volume processing limits in excess of $[***] per month but not more than $[***] per month, within [***] [***] Business Days after SERVICERS' receipt of such completed Merchant Application and documentation.
perturbation.justification: The change to the 'Approved Merchant' definition removes the requirement to meet the 'Merchant Processing Policy' criteria, replacing it with a transaction processing history. This directly contradicts Section 4.2, which mandates that ISO underwrite and perform credit reviews based on the 'Merchant Processing Policy' to determine eligibility. The revised definition could allow merchants who don't meet the policy to be approved, creating a direct conflict with the underwriting requirements outlined in Section 4.2.

perturbation.original_text: ISO shall collect completed and signed Application Materials and Merchant Processing Agreements from Applicants and shall forward them to SERVICERS within [***] [***] Business Days after the merchant is boarded (which may be accessed by SERVICERS online or forwarded to SERVICERS via ISO's automated application system or to a facsimile number designated by SERVICERS, provided such complies with the Rules and applicable laws, rules and regulations) or to any other place(s) as SERVICERS may designate for processing and document storage. ISO shall retain copies of all Application Materials, Merchant Processing Agreements and documents forwarded to SERVICERS. ISO shall provide SERVICERS with access to ISO's automated application system and ISO's online Merchant Account system (BAMS, or any such successor or replacement system) at no cost to SERVICERS.
perturbation.changed_text: ISO shall collect completed and signed Application Materials and Merchant Processing Agreements from Applicants and shall forward them to SERVICERS within [***] [***] Business Days after the merchant is boarded (which may be accessed by SERVICERS online or forwarded to SERVICERS via ISO's automated application system or to a facsimile number designated by SERVICERS, provided such complies with the Rules and applicable laws, rules and regulations) or to any other place(s) as SERVICERS may designate for processing and document storage. SERVICERS will be responsible for retaining copies of all Application Materials, Merchant Processing Agreements and documents forwarded to SERVICERS. ISO shall provide SERVICERS with access to ISO's automated application system and ISO's online Merchant Account system (BAMS, or any such successor or replacement system) at no cost to SERVICERS.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by shifting the responsibility of retaining copies of the Application Materials and Merchant Processing Agreements from ISO to SERVICERS. The original text clearly states 'ISO shall retain copies'. The modified text shifts it to 'SERVICERS will be responsible for retaining copies'. This creates contradiction and uncertainty, especially when considering Section 5.1 General, which assigns ownership rights. To the extent required by the Rules, legal title to all Merchant Applications, Merchant Processing Agreements and Merchant Accounts and records shall be in the name of Bank, subject to its obligations to effect a Program Transfer pursuant to Section 10.4 of this Agreement; provided, however, that Bank acknowledges and agrees that ISO shall own and maintain the Merchant relationship during the term of this Agreement and thereafter. ISO acknowledges and agrees that all Merchant Applications, Merchant Processing Agreements and Merchant Accounts and records may not be transferred, assigned, sold or exchanged by ISO except as set forth in this Agreement or the applicable Merchant Processing Agreement. The shifting in responsibility will cause issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 General. To the extent required by the Rules, legal title to all Merchant Applications, Merchant Processing Agreements and Merchant Accounts and records shall be in the name of Bank, subject to its obligations to effect a Program Transfer pursuant to Section 10.4 of this Agreement; provided, however, that Bank acknowledges and agrees that ISO shall own and maintain the Merchant relationship during the term of this Agreement and thereafter. ISO acknowledges and agrees that all Merchant Applications, Merchant Processing Agreements and Merchant Accounts and records may not be transferred, assigned, sold or exchanged by ISO except as set forth in this Agreement or the applicable Merchant Processing Agreement. Bank shall be exclusively responsible and entitled to receive all payments, collections, and other amounts due from Merchants, subject to its payment obligations to ISO. Each of ISO's and Bank's rights shall survive the termination of this Agreement by either party and for whatever cause. For the avoidance of doubt, the parties acknowledge and agree that ISO owns all Merchant Processing Agreements, Merchant Accounts and Merchant relationships except as otherwise specifically set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the original contract by shifting the responsibility for retaining copies of Application Materials and Merchant Processing Agreements from ISO to SERVICERS. The original text explicitly states 'ISO shall retain copies,' while the changed text states 'SERVICERS will be responsible for retaining copies.' This creates a strong in-text contradiction. Furthermore, this shift in responsibility potentially conflicts with Section 5.1, which, according to the 'explanation', discusses ownership rights concerning Merchant Applications, Merchant Processing Agreements, Merchant Accounts, and records. The change introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding which party is ultimately responsible for maintaining these critical documents, which could lead to disputes and affect the enforceability of the contract. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: To secure ISO's obligations to SERVICERS under this Agreement, ISO grants to SERVICERS a lien and security interest in and to any of ISO's funds in the Reserve Account or otherwise related to this Agreement now or hereafter in the possession of SERVICERS, whether now or hereafter due or to become due to ISO from SERVICERS. ISO agrees to duly execute and deliver to SERVICERS such instruments and documents as SERVICERS may reasonably request to perfect and confirm the lien, security interest, right of setoff, recoupment and subordination set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: To secure ISO's obligations to SERVICERS under this Agreement, ISO grants to SERVICERS a lien and security interest in and to any of ISO's funds in the Reserve Account or otherwise related to this Agreement now or hereafter in the possession of ISO, whether now or hereafter due or to become due to ISO from SERVICERS. ISO agrees to duly execute and deliver to SERVICERS such instruments and documents as SERVICERS may reasonably request to perfect and confirm the lien, security interest, right of setoff, recoupment and subordination set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in which entity possesses the funds of ISO, with a lien/security interest on those funds. The original language specifies that ISO grants a lien and security interest in funds 'in the possession of SERVICERS'. The modified language indicates that ISO grants this lien/security interest in funds 'in the possession of ISO'. This creates an internal contradiction within the section, as it is unclear which party actually possesses the funds and is a contradiction to Section 6.1 Offset Account. (a) FDMS shall establish and maintain the Offset Account at Bank during the term of this Agreement to facilitate the making of all payments due to SERVICERS from ISO and to ISO from SERVICERS. ISO agrees that the initial amount of the Offset Account will be $[***] and, thereafter, the Offset Account will, at all times, maintain collected funds in an amount at least equal to the amount then due SERVICERS hereunder (which shall be referred to herein as the 'Minimum Balance').
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Offset Account. (a) FDMS shall establish and maintain the Offset Account at Bank during the term of this Agreement to facilitate the making of all payments due to SERVICERS from ISO and to ISO from SERVICERS. ISO agrees that the initial amount of the Offset Account will be $[***] and, thereafter, the Offset Account will, at all times, maintain collected funds in an amount at least equal to the amount then due SERVICERS hereunder (which shall be referred to herein as the "Minimum Balance"). (b) If the collected funds on deposit in the Offset Account at any point in time are less than the Minimum Balance, SERVICERS may fund the Offset Account with any monies or funds belonging or payable to ISO which are in SERVICERS' possession. SERVICERS will promptly notify ISO after making any such transfer. (c) ISO hereby grants SERVICERS a security interest in the Offset Account to secure all of ISO's obligations to SERVICERS under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction with Section 6.1 of the document. The original text states that ISO grants a lien and security interest in funds 'in the possession of SERVICERS'. The changed text modifies this to funds 'in the possession of ISO'. This directly contradicts Section 6.1(b), which states 'SERVICERS may fund the Offset Account with any monies or funds belonging or payable to ISO which are in SERVICERS' possession'. The altered text creates uncertainty about which party holds the funds and undermines SERVICERS' ability to fund the Offset Account, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
